body:
## Summary

Hybrid optimization can report `Best score: 0.0000` (and `Acc 0.0000`) even when quality metrics are present and non-zero.

Observed in `examples/hybrid_mode_demo/run_mastra_js_optimization.py` when objective is `accuracy` but hybrid API returns `text_accuracy` and/or `tool_accuracy` without a canonical `accuracy` key.

## Reproduction

```bash
TRAIGENT_EXECUTION_MODE=hybrid \
TRAIGENT_COST_APPROVED=true \
MASTRA_JS_MAX_TRIALS=10 \
MASTRA_JS_MAX_COST_USD=4.0 \
.venv/bin/python examples/hybrid_mode_demo/run_mastra_js_optimization.py
```

Example output:

- `Best score: 0.0000`
- `All trials: Acc 0.0000`
- `Best metrics` contains non-zero metrics:
  - `tool_accuracy: 1.0000`
  - `text_accuracy: 0.5000`

Also often seen in same runs:

- `Batch execution failed: Request timed out: HTTP 504`
- `success_rate: 0.3333`

Note: timeout-driven `success_rate` reduction is operational reliability and separate from the scoring-key bug.

## Root Cause

The demo optimizes `objectives=["accuracy"]`, but `HybridAPIEvaluator` only forwards aggregated keys that exist in response metrics. If API emits split quality keys (`text_accuracy`, `tool_accuracy`) and no `accuracy`, objective lookup misses and score collapses to `0.0`.

## Expected

When objective is `accuracy`, hybrid runs should still produce a meaningful score if `*_accuracy` metrics are present.

## Actual

No canonical `accuracy` is emitted, objective scoring resolves to zero.

## Proposed Fix

In `traigent/evaluators/hybrid_api.py`:

1. Infer canonical `accuracy` when missing from available `*_accuracy` metrics (prefer `text_accuracy`, `tool_accuracy`, `overall_accuracy`, then fallback to any `*_accuracy`).
2. Set `score` from canonical `accuracy` when missing.
3. Add `response_time_ms` alias from `latency` for UI compatibility.

## Suggested Tests

`tests/unit/evaluators/test_hybrid_api_evaluator.py`

- infers `accuracy` + `score` from `text_accuracy` + `tool_accuracy`
- preserves explicit `accuracy` when already present
- adds `response_time_ms` when latency is present

## Scope

SDK-only, backward compatible, no hybrid API contract change required.
title: Hybrid evaluator: objective=accuracy scores 0.0000 when API returns split accuracy metrics

body:
## Summary
When a Traigent Hybrid API server is deployed behind Cloudflare or similar WAF/CDN (e.g., `ai.bazak.ai`), HTTP requests made with Python's `requests` library are rejected with `403 Forbidden`. The root cause is that Cloudflare's bot protection blocks the default `User-Agent: python-requests/2.x.x` header.

## Reproduction

```bash
# This works (curl sends User-Agent: curl/x.x)
curl -s https://ai.bazak.ai/traigent/v1/health
# => {"status":"healthy","version":"1.0.0"}

# This fails (python-requests sends User-Agent: python-requests/2.32.3)
python3 -c "import requests; r = requests.get('https://ai.bazak.ai/traigent/v1/health'); print(r.status_code, r.text[:100])"
# => 403 <!doctype html>...403 Forbidden

# This works (override User-Agent)
python3 -c "import requests; r = requests.get('https://ai.bazak.ai/traigent/v1/health', headers={'User-Agent': 'Traigent-SDK/1.0'}); print(r.status_code)"
# => 200
```

## Problem
- The SDK's `HybridAPIEvaluator` uses `httpx` with `User-Agent: Traigent-SDK/1.0` (set in `traigent/hybrid/http_transport.py`), so the SDK itself is **not affected**.
- However, anyone writing quick validation scripts, examples, or tests using `requests` will silently get 403 with no indication that User-Agent is the cause.
- The 403 error page is HTML, not JSON, so `response.json()` also crashes — compounding confusion.

## Scope
**In scope:**
- Document the User-Agent requirement for CDN-fronted servers
- Add User-Agent header to all example scripts using `requests`
- Consider adding a note in Hybrid API integration docs

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Cloudflare WAF rules (server-side concern)
- Modifying the SDK's httpx transport (already correct)

## Proposed Fix
1. Add `"User-Agent": "Traigent-SDK/1.0"` to all example scripts that use `requests`
2. Add a troubleshooting note in Hybrid API docs: "If you get 403 behind Cloudflare, set a custom User-Agent"
3. Consider a helper function in examples: `get_headers(token)` that always includes the correct UA

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All example scripts in `examples/hybrid_mode_demo/` include `User-Agent` header
- [ ] Hybrid API integration guide mentions CDN/WAF User-Agent requirement
- [ ] `test_mastra_js_api.py` works against CDN-fronted servers without modification

## Priority
**P2** — Not an SDK bug (httpx transport is fine), but causes silent failures for users writing integration scripts.
title: bug(hybrid): Cloudflare/WAF blocks python-requests User-Agent on Hybrid API servers behind CDN

body:
## Summary

The backend has an internal validation inconsistency: the JSON schema at the API boundary (`SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA`) allows up to **1000** metric keys, but the Pydantic `MeasuresDict` business-logic validator caps at **50**. A request with 51–999 metric keys passes the API gate but fails deeper in the stack, producing a confusing error path for callers.

## Problem

| Layer | Max metric keys | Enforced? |
|---|---|---|
| `src/schemas/traigent.py` — `SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA` | 1000 | Yes (API boundary) |
| `src/models/workflow_metadata.py` — `MeasuresDict` | 50 | Yes (business logic) |
| SDK `MeasuresDict` | 50 | Yes (client-side) |
| SQLAlchemy `ConfigurationRun.measures` | unlimited | No (JSON column) |

Since both the SDK and backend Pydantic agree on 50, the JSON schema's 1000 limit is the outlier. Users (or future clients) that trust the schema contract can craft valid-looking requests that silently fail at the service layer.

## Scope

**In scope:** Aligning the `maxProperties` value in `SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA` with the `MeasuresDict` limit.

**Out of scope:** TraigentSchema alignment (tracked separately), SQLAlchemy-level enforcement.

## Files

- `src/schemas/traigent.py` — `SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA` `metrics` field (`maxProperties: 1000`)
- `src/models/workflow_metadata.py` — `MeasuresDict` (`MAX_MEASURES_COUNT = 50`)

## Proposed Fix

Lower `maxProperties` for the `metrics` field in `SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA` from 1000 to 50 to match the Pydantic and SDK contract. This is a one-line change:

```python
# src/schemas/traigent.py — SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA → metrics
"maxProperties": 50  # was 1000
```

Alternatively, if 1000 is intentional for bulk/aggregate metrics (distinct from per-example `MeasuresDict`), document why the limits differ and ensure the error message at the Pydantic layer is clear.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA` and `MeasuresDict` agree on the max metric keys limit
- [ ] A request with >50 metric keys is rejected at the API boundary with a clear 400 error (not a 500 from Pydantic)
- [ ] Existing tests updated to cover the boundary (50 keys passes, 51 fails)

## Priority

**P2** — Not user-facing today (SDK enforces 50 client-side), but creates a latent bug for any non-SDK caller trusting the schema.
title: bug(validation): SUBMIT_RESULTS_SCHEMA allows 1000 metric keys but MeasuresDict caps at 50

body:
## Summary
`traigent auth whoami` can report an API key as invalid when the actual issue is backend endpoint mismatch (`404`) or connectivity failure.  
This is a CLI diagnostics issue only; optimization runtime is unaffected.

## Actual Reproduction
```bash
export TRAIGENT_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:5000
unset TRAIGENT_API_URL
.venv/bin/traigent auth whoami "$TRAIGENT_API_KEY"
```

Observed output:
```
❌ Invalid or expired API key
Backend: http://localhost:5000/api/v1
HTTP status: 404
Response: {
```

This indicates endpoint mismatch, not necessarily invalid credentials.

## Problem
Current `whoami` handling is too binary and can misclassify:
- endpoint mismatch (`404`) as invalid key
- backend unavailability as raw exception/traceback (uncaught `aiohttp.ClientError` path)

This causes unnecessary key rotation and confusing support/debug loops.

## Scope
**In scope:**
- `auth_commands.py` (`whoami` diagnostics + error handling)

**Out of scope:**
- optimization execution/runtime
- backend auth model
- key generation UX
- cloud trial submission/analytics

## Proposed Fix (Preferred: Option A, minimal)
Keep single endpoint behavior, improve classification:
- `200` → valid key
- `401/403` → invalid/unauthorized key
- `404` → endpoint mismatch message (not "invalid key")
- `aiohttp.ClientError` / timeout → connectivity message, no traceback

## Alternative (Option B, not preferred for now)
Endpoint/header discovery fallback (multiple endpoints + both header styles).  
**Note:** this was prototyped and reviewed; downside is up to 6 sequential requests in worst-case failure and currently no dedicated tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `whoami` does not label `404` as invalid key.
- [ ] `whoami` gracefully handles `aiohttp.ClientError` and timeout (no raw Python traceback).
- [ ] Output always includes backend URL and diagnostic category.
- [ ] Unit tests cover `200`, `401`, `403`, `404`, timeout/network error paths.
- [ ] No changes to optimization runtime behavior.

## Priority
**Low** (CLI UX/diagnostics only; no optimization correctness impact).
title: CLI `auth whoami` misclassifies backend mismatch/connectivity as invalid API key

body:
## Summary

`HybridAPIEvaluator` does not set `EvaluationResult.summary_stats`, even though it already has per-example data (`HybridExampleResult`) needed to compute describe-style statistics.

This creates analytics parity gaps versus local evaluator output.

## Why this matters

In local mode, `summary_stats` is generated from per-example metrics and attached to the result:
- `traigent/evaluators/metrics_tracker.py:348`
- `traigent/evaluators/local.py:1217`
- `traigent/evaluators/local.py:1262`

In hybrid/edge mode, `HybridAPIEvaluator` returns `EvaluationResult` without `summary_stats`:
- `traigent/evaluators/hybrid_api.py:287`
- `traigent/evaluators/hybrid_api.py:383`

The backend can derive fallback summary payloads from flat `metrics` when missing:
- `TraigentBackend/src/services/traigent/session_service_refactored.py:616`
- `TraigentBackend/src/services/traigent/session_service_refactored.py:681`

…but that is not equivalent to full describe-style per-metric distributions from per-example data.

## Contract clarification

This is **not** a hybrid API contract bug.

Hybrid protocol/OpenAPI define `aggregate_metrics`, not `summary_stats`:
- `traigent/hybrid/protocol.py:184`
- `traigent-api/openapi/components/schemas/EvaluateResponse.yaml:3`

So this fix belongs in SDK evaluator internals, not in external JS/HTTP implementors.

## Current data flow

```
HybridAPIEvaluator.evaluate()
  -> builds list[HybridExampleResult] (cost_usd, latency_ms, metrics, success)
  -> _compute_aggregated_metrics() (means only)
  -> returns EvaluationResult(summary_stats=None)
      -> trial_result_factory copies summary_stats only if present
      -> metadata_helpers adds summary_stats only if present
```

Relevant files:
- `traigent/evaluators/hybrid_api.py:43`
- `traigent/evaluators/hybrid_api.py:701`
- `traigent/core/trial_result_factory.py:90`
- `traigent/core/metadata_helpers.py:145`

## What is lacking if not fixed

- Hybrid trials lack local-parity summary distributions (`count/mean/std/min/25/50/75/max`) derived from per-example results.
- Analytics features relying on higher-fidelity summary fields can degrade to fallbacks.
- We already downgraded missing-summary logs to debug (UX fix), but data parity remains unresolved.

## Proposed fix

### Scope
- `traigent/evaluators/hybrid_api.py`
- optional extraction of stats helper from `traigent/evaluators/metrics_tracker.py:471` into shared utility (or duplicate logic with tests)

### Implementation
1. Add `_build_summary_stats(results, duration)` in `HybridAPIEvaluator`.
2. Build describe-style stats from available per-example data:
   - `accuracy`
   - `cost` / `total_cost` alias
   - `latency`
   - `success_rate`
   - additional custom per-example numeric metrics
3. Set `summary_stats=...` in `EvaluationResult(...)` return from `evaluate()`.

### Non-goals
- No hybrid protocol/OpenAPI changes.
- No JS-Mastra changes required.

## Suggested acceptance criteria

- Hybrid evaluator returns non-null `EvaluationResult.summary_stats` for successful runs.
- Shape is pandas-describe compatible per metric.
- `total_examples` and metadata are populated.
- Existing local evaluator behavior unchanged.
- New/updated tests:
  - `tests/unit/evaluators/test_hybrid_api.py` for summary_stats presence + shape
  - integration coverage for hybrid summary propagation to trial metadata

## Replication steps (before fix)

1. Run hybrid demo against working JS API:

```bash
MASTRA_JS_TUNABLE_ID=child-age-agent-a \
MASTRA_JS_MAX_TRIALS=2 \
MASTRA_JS_DATASET_SIZE=4 \
TRAIGENT_COST_APPROVED=true \
TRAIGENT_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG \
.venv/bin/python examples/hybrid_mode_demo/run_mastra_js_optimization.py
```

2. Observe SDK debug line from trial submit path indicating missing optional summary payload:
- `traigent/cloud/trial_operations.py:454`

3. Inspect evaluator return path in code (`hybrid_api.py`) and confirm no `summary_stats` assignment.

## Priority

Low-Medium (no optimization correctness impact, but analytics fidelity gap for hybrid mode).
title: Hybrid evaluator should generate summary_stats from per-example results

body:
## Context

Closed Traigent/traigent-api#2 — the API spec validates the contract level: `/v1/execute` is synchronous with `timeout_ms` and `408` timeout response.

The remaining question is whether the **Traigent SDK client** properly handles these potentially long-running synchronous calls:

## What to validate

1. **Async I/O**: Does the SDK use async HTTP calls (e.g., `httpx.AsyncClient`) so the optimization loop isn't blocked while waiting for `/v1/execute` to return?
2. **Timeout enforcement**: Does the SDK pass `timeout_ms` to the HTTP client and handle `408` responses gracefully?
3. **Parallel trials**: When running parallel trials via `ThreadPoolExecutor`, does each thread's execute call respect its own timeout budget independently?
4. **Partial failure handling**: If one input in a batch times out (`batch_options.timeout_per_item_ms`), does the SDK correctly process the `ExecutionError.failed_inputs` response?

## Key files

- `traigent/core/orchestrator.py` — optimization loop, parallel trial execution
- `traigent/cloud/` — HTTP client implementation
- API spec: `timeout_ms` field in `ExecuteRequest`, `408` response, `batch_options.timeout_per_item_ms`

## Origin

Traigent/traigent-api#2
title: Validate SDK async handling for /v1/execute calls

body:
## OpenAPI YAML — Remaining Gaps After PR #108 (ref: Issue #107)

Verified PR #108 diff against `docs/hybrid-mode-openapi.yaml`. The markdown docs (contract, client guide) are well-addressed. However, 3 YAML-specific gaps remain that affect Swagger UI rendering — which is how an implementer (human or AI) consuming the spec will see the API.

These are additive to the PR #108 fixes, not contradictions.

---

### 1. Inline examples still show full-content, not ID-based

**Schema paths:**
- `paths./traigent/v1/execute.post.requestBody.content.application/json.example`
- `paths./traigent/v1/evaluate.post.requestBody.content.application/json.example`

**Current state in PR #108:** Unchanged from main. The `/traigent/v1/execute` example sends `data: {query: "What is machine learning?"}` and the `/traigent/v1/evaluate` example sends `output: {response: "..."}` + `target: {expected: "..."}` inline.

**Problem:** Swagger UI renders these as the default "Try it out" payloads. An implementer copying them builds for full-content mode. The schema descriptions now correctly say "Full-content datasets" (thanks to Fix 1.3), but the examples contradict by showing full-content as the primary pattern.

**Evidence:** `examples/hybrid_mode_demo/run_bazak_optimization.py:99` sends only IDs:
```python
EvaluationExample(input_data={"input_id": f"case_{i:03d}"})
```

**Suggested fix:** Swap primary examples to ID-based:
```yaml
# /traigent/v1/execute
inputs:
  - input_id: "case_001"
    # data omitted — server looks up actual input locally by input_id

# /traigent/v1/evaluate
evaluations:
  - input_id: "case_001"
    output_id: "out_case_001_exec_abc"
    # output/target omitted — server looks up both locally.
    # target_id is optional: SDK only sends it when the dataset
    # explicitly contains target references (hybrid_api.py:583-586).
```

---

### 2. `quality_metrics` YAML description doesn't explain two-phase vs combined

**Schema path:** `components.schemas.ExecuteResponse.properties.quality_metrics`

**Current state in PR #108:** Description is still `"Quality metrics if using combined mode"`. The detailed two-phase vs combined explanation (Fix 1.1) was added to `hybrid-mode-api-contract.md` and `hybrid-mode-client-guide.md` — but not to the YAML schema description itself.

**Problem:** A Swagger UI reader sees only "Quality metrics if using combined mode" with no explanation of what combined mode means or that returning null/empty triggers a separate `/traigent/v1/evaluate` call.

**Nuance on truthiness:** The contract table says "`quality_metrics` is non-null (contains data)" — this is functionally correct but could be more precise. The SDK uses Python truthiness (`if execute_response.quality_metrics:` at `hybrid_api.py:449`), so `quality_metrics: {}` (empty object) is falsy and still triggers two-phase mode. Only a **non-empty** object (e.g., `{"accuracy": 0.95}`) triggers combined mode.

**Suggested fix:** Expand the YAML description:
```yaml
quality_metrics:
  type: object
  description: |
    Quality metrics from evaluation (combined execute+evaluate mode).
    Controls whether the SDK calls /traigent/v1/evaluate separately:
    - null, omitted, or empty ({}) → SDK calls /traigent/v1/evaluate (two-phase, recommended)
    - non-empty object with metrics → SDK uses these directly, skips /traigent/v1/evaluate
  additionalProperties:
    type: number
  nullable: true
```

---

### 3. Endpoint descriptions lack server-side implementation guidance

**Schema paths:**
- `paths./traigent/v1/execute.post.description`
- `paths./traigent/v1/evaluate.post.description`

**Current state in PR #108:** Descriptions unchanged. The `/traigent/v1/execute` description says "Execute the agent with a specific configuration on a batch of inputs." The `/traigent/v1/evaluate` description says "Evaluate outputs against expected targets to measure quality." Neither explains what the server should **do** in privacy-preserving mode.

**Problem:** The schema descriptions on `InputItem` and `EvaluationItem` mention privacy-preserving mode, but endpoint descriptions are what Swagger UI shows prominently at the top. An AI agent generating code from this spec will build a passthrough expecting the caller to send all data.

**Suggested fix:** Add implementation notes to endpoint descriptions:
```yaml
# /traigent/v1/execute
description: |
  Execute the agent with a specific configuration on a batch of inputs.
  This is the main endpoint where your agent processes inputs using the provided configuration.

  **Server implementation (privacy-preserving mode):**
  1. For each input, use `input_id` to look up the actual query from your local dataset
  2. Run your agent with the provided `config` parameters
  3. Store the response locally, keyed by a generated `output_id`
  4. Return `output_id` + cost/latency per input (do NOT return response text)

# /traigent/v1/evaluate
description: |
  Evaluate outputs against expected targets to measure quality.
  This endpoint is optional — only implement if supports_evaluate is true in capabilities.

  **Server implementation (privacy-preserving mode):**
  1. For each evaluation item, use `output_id` to look up the stored response locally
  2. Use `input_id` to look up the expected behavior from your local dataset
  3. Compare and compute quality metrics
  4. Return per-example metrics and aggregate statistics (mean, std, n)
```

---

### Summary

| Gap | Schema Path | PR #108 Status | Severity |
|-----|------------|---------------|----------|
| Examples show full-content | `paths.*.requestBody...example` | Unchanged | High — Swagger "Try it out" uses these |
| `quality_metrics` description minimal | `components.schemas.ExecuteResponse.properties.quality_metrics` | Unchanged | Medium — two-phase explanation only in contract MD |
| No server-side guidance | `paths.*.post.description` | Unchanged | Medium — implementers don't know what to DO |

All three copies need the same fixes: `docs/hybrid-mode-openapi.yaml`, `docs/bazak/hybrid-mode-openapi.yaml`, `docs/bazak2/hybrid-mode-openapi.yaml`.

These can be folded into PR #108 before merge, or tracked as a follow-up.
title: OpenAPI YAML: inline examples and descriptions still show full-content after PR #108
